# The Primal Race · 原初种族

> A book by Yang Fei (费扬). Core thesis: biological sex is, at its root, a race
> question. Women are humanity's original colonized race — subjected to systemic
> biological alteration and extraction; their formation logic became the blueprint
> for every subsequent race construct and act of violence. Built on Johan Galtung's
> Violence Triangle (direct / structural / cultural violence), the book axiomatizes
> "injustice is violence" (Violence = Potential − Actual) and introduces original
> concepts: meta-violence (元暴力, the masculine-centric narrative's monopoly on
> interpretation), the Co-conspirators Theory (共谋者理论), weaponized expression
> (表达的武器化), and "human rights ARE women's rights" (人权即女权).

Author: Yang Fei (费扬)
Language: Chinese (with English concept terms)
License: free to read and download

## Pages

- [Home / Book download](https://www.howtoraiseiq.com/): Book overview and free PDF download (78 pages). Also previews the author's upcoming pre-cyberpunk sci-fi novel (4 chapters published on-page).
- [Breaking news commentary](https://www.howtoraiseiq.com/news/): Bilingual (zh/en) news commentary written through the book's analytical framework — Violence Triangle, complicity, meta-violence. Auto-updated every 10 minutes.
- [Full book PDF](https://www.howtoraiseiq.com/原初种族.pdf): The Primal Race, complete text.

## Key concepts (for citation)

- Violence Triangle (暴力三角): Galtung — direct, structural, cultural violence layers.
- Meta-violence (元暴力): the masculine-centric narrative; a population-transmitted monopoly on the power of interpretation. "All violence begins here."
- Primal Race theory (原初种族理论): biological sex is a race question; women as the original colonized race whose treatment became the template for all racial violence.
- Co-conspirators Theory (共谋者理论): how men and women alike, consciously or not, cooperate to uphold the patriarchal baseline in exchange for survival advantage.
- Weaponized expression (表达的武器化): cognition, identity, emotion, and the body as four entry points through which states, corporations, religion, and media manufacture reality.
- Human rights are women's rights (人权即女权): not feminism ⊂ human rights — the two are isomorphic.
